creator: oliver@FreeBSD.org
text:
when NO_OPENSSL = YES is specified, world does not compile. I've the following
	make options set in make.conf:

NO_KERBEROS="YES"
NO_BLUETOOTH="YES"
NO_CVS="YES"
NO_DICT="YES"
NO_FORTRAN="YES"
NO_GPIB="YES"
NO_I4B="YES"
NO_IPFILTER="YES"
NO_PF="YES"
NO_AUTHPF="YES"
NO_INET6="YES"
NO_ATM="YES"
NO_USB="YES"
NO_LPR="YES"
NO_ACPI="YES"
NO_MAILWRAPPER="YES"
NO_NETCAT="YES"
NO_NIS="YES"
NO_OPENSSH="YES"
NO_OPENSSL="YES"
NO_SENDMAIL="YES"
NO_SHAREDOCS="YES"
NO_CRYPT="YES"
NO_GAMES="YES"
NO_INFO="YES"
NO_BIND="YES"
PPP_NO_NAT="YES"
PPP_NO_NETGRAP="YES"
PPP_NO_RADIUS="YES"
PPP_NO_SUID="YES"
NO_RCMDS="YES"

	and this is the error I get:

How-To-Repeat: 	set my make options posted above
